entry.title: How to identify test data in Open Data views?
entry.content:
May I know how can I distinguish testmail receiver from the email open data?

I want to collect the email performance data of customers who receive the email after email campaign launch, excluding the testmail data.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to identify test data in Open Data views?
entry.content: A possible way is to link email campaigns V2 view and performance data view using a JOIN (send, click, bounce, others) and filter out the entries which "campaign_type = test".

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content:
BananaCode

Code with friends  , debugging  with pets !

entry.title: Fields empty After Saving and empty in Executive review
entry.content:
Hi all,

i might be stupid but I configured a worksheet were the final annual Salary will be summarized for Austrian People different than for others. For Austrians there are 14 monthly salaries, versus 12 for all others.  
I have a Stringfield „geozone“ where I can refer to. 
the Formula for the Final annual Salary Looks Like this: 

If(geozone=‚AUT‘,cust_newmonthlysalary*12+cust_currentmonthlysalary*2,cust_newmonthlysalary*10+cust_currentmonthlysalary*2)

when i type in the Salary increase all fields are calculating correctly. But After i Save the sheet or when i want to Export via Executive Review, the respective Field with the yearly Salary is empty. 
can anybody please give me an idea what could be the matter?  

Thank you in advance 🙂

 

#compensation #successfactors #executivereview

entry.title: Re: #COE KKS01 Business Partner (A2X) API response doesn't return person field
entry.content: This issue is solved?

entry.title: Significance of WCM Objects
entry.content:
Hi All, 

Greetings !

Can any one please explain the significance of below WCM objects ? What are the usages of these objects?

1. Valuation 

2. Work Approval 

3. Work Clearance Application 

4. Work Clearance Document 

5. Additional Application 

Thanking you very much .

Best Regards

Mohammed

entry.title: Customer & Partner Roundtable for SAP BTP ABAP Environment #17
entry.content:
👉 The call for contributions for one of the upcoming roundtables is open! 

 

If you want to show a demo or share a use case scenario for SAP BTP ABAP Environment send us an email and we will get back to you.
 

Introduction

 

As SAP BTP ABAP environment (aka Steampunk) and ABAP Cloud became more popular inside and outside of SAP, there is a high demand for rolling out the latest product news and updates, asking questions, and showing demos. 

💡 You can find the slides presented, recordings, and further references from the previous rroundtables in this GitHub repository.


Meeting Information

When:                                                                      
  • October 22nd, 10:00 - 11:00 AM CET  – Zoom Meeting (please register in advance) 
 
Who:
  • All interested customers, partners, and stakeholders are invited to join this roundtable
  • Steampunk team: Frank Jentsch (Product Lead for SAP BTP ABAP Environment), Iwona Hahn, Burcu Karlidag, Ronny Pahlke (Product Management)

Preliminary Agenda:
  • System landscape setup and costs
  • Pre-upgrade option for 2411 release
  • Planned features for 2411 release
  • Q&A 
Looking forward to meeting you!
 


Check out our SAP Business Technology ABAP Environment page in SAP Community for product updates and upcoming events.

entry.title: Re: openSAP has moved: Find over 100 former openSAP courses on the SAP Learning site
entry.content:
@PRR You are looking for two former openSAP courses. 

  • The course "SAP Focused Run – Operations of Hybrid Landscapes" has been migrated to the SAP Learning site. You'll find it here: https://learning.sap.com/courses/sap-focused-run-operations-of-hybrid-landscapes 
  • The course "Managing Java Systems on SAP Netweaver" was already six years old. The content has not been migrated and is not available any longer.

entry.title: Automating SAP Post-Refresh Steps with SAP GUI Scripting - Part 1
entry.content:
To help you automate SAP post-refresh steps, I'll provide an outline based on commonly used SAP T-codes like SU01, SM21, SE06, SCC4, RZ10, RZ04, and AL11. These are essential T-codes that come into play after refreshing an SAP system. In this article, I will describe how to automate each step using code examples (e.g., scripts or SAP Automation Frameworks such as SAP GUI scripting or ABAP) and explain how it helps reduce manual effort and human errors.

1. Understanding Post-Refresh Steps and Manual Effort

Post-refresh steps in SAP are crucial to ensure that a newly refreshed system (usually from production to quality or development) functions correctly with the required configurations. These steps often include resetting or configuring system settings, adjusting roles and authorizations, and cleaning up logs and profiles.

Manual execution of these steps across multiple systems leads to:

  • Human errors: Mistakes can easily occur, such as setting incorrect parameters or missing a step.
  • Repetitive tasks: Doing the same post-refresh tasks in each system is tedious and time-consuming.
  • Lack of consistency: Different individuals performing these tasks may interpret instructions differently.

By automating post-refresh steps, we reduce manual interaction, ensuring consistent and accurate system configurations, saving time, and avoiding errors.

2. Automation for Each T-Code

Let's now explore automation for each T-code from the provided file, including script examples and how they reduce manual work.

T-Code: SU01 - User Management (Role Adjustment)

Automation Script Example (SAP GUI Scripting):

vbs
Set session = SAPGuiApp.Children(0).Children(0)
session.StartTransaction "SU01"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtS_USER").Text = "USERNAME"
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[1]/btn[8]").Press 'Display user
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/subPERSONAS_AREA:SAPLSUU5:0300/txtSU01_ROLES").Text = "NEW_ROLE" 'Assign new role
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/btn[11]").Press 'Save changes

How It Helps:

  • Automates role assignment and user management.
  • Ensures all team members have correct roles in the refreshed system.
  • Reduces errors like assigning wrong roles to users.

T-Code: SM21 - System Log (Log Cleanup)

Automation Script Example (ABAP Report to Delete Logs):

abap
CALL FUNCTION 'RSSM_LOG_DELETE'
  EXPORTING
    log_number = 'ALL' " Deletes all logs
  EXCEPTIONS
    OTHERS = 1.

How It Helps:

  • Automatically cleans up logs after a refresh.
  • Reduces time spent manually reviewing and deleting unnecessary logs.
  • Ensures that the system is fresh with no outdated logs.

T-Code: SE06 - System Change Option (System Change Configuration)

Automation Script Example (SAP GUI Scripting):

vbs
Set session = SAPGuiApp.Children(0).Children(0)
session.StartTransaction "SE06"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/radRAD_MOD_ON").Select
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/btn[0]").Press 'Enable modifications for the system

How It Helps:

  • Automates system change settings.
  • Ensures that system configurations are set correctly after the refresh.
  • Reduces the risk of forgetting to unlock or lock the system for changes.

T-Code: SCC4 - Client Administration (Client Configuration)

Automation Script Example (SAP GUI Scripting):

vbs
Set session = SAPGuiApp.Children(0).Children(0)
session.StartTransaction "SCC4"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/lbl[0]").Press 'Select client
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtPFL_0010-CLIENT_ROLE").Text = "PRODUCTION" 'Set client role
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/btn[11]").Press 'Save changes

How It Helps:

  • Automates the process of setting client roles.
  • Ensures that client-specific settings are accurate after refresh, avoiding improper client roles.
  • Saves time by setting roles programmatically instead of manually.

T-Code: RZ10 - Profile Parameter Management

Automation Script Example (ABAP Script to Change Profile Parameters):

abap
CALL FUNCTION 'RZ10_PARM_MAINTAIN'
  EXPORTING
    profile_name = 'DEFAULT.PFL'
    parameter_name = 'rdisp/mshost'
    parameter_value = 'new_value'
  EXCEPTIONS
    OTHERS = 1.

How It Helps:

  • Ensures that all necessary profile parameters are correctly updated post-refresh.
  • Reduces manual errors in setting parameters by automating the process.
  • Can be scheduled to update multiple parameters at once, saving time.

T-Code: RZ04 - Operation Modes and Instances

Automation Script Example (SAP GUI Scripting):

vbs
Set session = SAPGuiApp.Children(0).Children(0)
session.StartTransaction "RZ04"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/txtS_OPER_MODE").Text = "DEFAULT_MODE"
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/btn[11]").Press 'Save operation mode

How It Helps:

  • Automates the configuration of operation modes.
  • Ensures that the system switches to the correct operation modes without manual intervention.
  • Reduces the likelihood of incorrect settings that could affect system performance.

T-Code: AL11 - Directory Display

Automation Script Example (SAP GUI Scripting for Directory Check):

vbs
Set session = SAPGuiApp.Children(0).Children(0)
session.StartTransaction "AL11"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtDIR_NAME").Text = "/usr/sap/trans" 'Change to required directory
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/btn[11]").Press 'Display contents of the directory

How It Helps:

  • Automatically checks directory contents to ensure that post-refresh transport or system files are available.
  • Reduces the time spent manually navigating and checking directories.
  • Can be scheduled to run periodically to check directory consistency.

3. Benefits of Automation

By automating these post-refresh steps, several key benefits can be realized:

  • Error Reduction: Automation ensures that each step is executed correctly every time, reducing the likelihood of human error.
  • Time Savings: Scripts run much faster than manual intervention, particularly when dealing with large systems.
  • Consistency: Automated steps ensure consistent execution across systems, leading to predictable outcomes.
  • Audit Trail: Automation scripts can be logged, providing an auditable record of actions taken.

4. Conclusion

Automating SAP post-refresh steps such as user management (SU01), log cleanup (SM21), system change configurations (SE06), and profile updates (RZ10) can significantly reduce the manual effort and errors associated with post-refresh tasks. By using SAP GUI scripting or ABAP automation, these processes can be streamlined, ensuring consistent, accurate, and fast post-refresh operations. Organizations implementing automation for these steps will benefit from increased system stability and reduced downtime.

entry.title: Re: Generative AI with SAP – Part 4 – AI Foundation on SAP BTP
entry.content: Very informative post. Thank you for sharing!

entry.title: Emarsys need to generate API key
entry.content:
We need to generate API keys in both sandbox and production Emarsys. But we don't see the options available to us that the documentation describes. Does something need to be turned on in order to enable the API functionality? Thanks


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Emarsys need to generate API key
entry.content:
According to the following SAP Emarsys documentation, you must generate a X-WSSE header based on the API user/pw that Account Owners can create in the platform to authenticate API calls: Authentication | Emarsys Core API Guides

This X-WSSE header should be generated based on the code examples provided at the section "Computing the Password Digest" of the document above. The algorithm generates the entire header based on the Emarsys suite, API user and API password.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano 

My name is Shaheer Farrukh. I belong to the following groups:

  • People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts
  • First generation college students
  • People who lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for themselves

I’m an enthusiastic bachelor’s student with a keen interest in pursuing a career as an SAP consultant. My academic journey has provided me with a solid foundation in business principles and information technology, fueling my passion for how innovative software solutions can transform organizations.

Throughout my studies, I have developed a strong understanding of ERP systems and their impact on business processes. I’m eager to apply my knowledge and skills in real-world scenarios, helping companies streamline their operations and enhance decision-making through SAP solutions. I thrive in collaborative environments and am excited about the opportunity to learn from experienced professionals while contributing to meaningful projects.

As I continue my education, I am actively seeking internships and opportunities to gain hands-on experience in SAP consulting, further developing my technical expertise and problem-solving abilities. I am committed to continuous learning and am excited about the dynamic challenges this field offers.

I look forward to connecting with like-minded professionals and exploring the endless possibilities within the world of SAP!

entry.title: Re: ML_GET_BLOCKED_INFO
entry.content: I guess I need to provide more background on this issue... Originally, our client database was being used to synchronize handhelds uaing Sybase 9.0 for our FAST schema. When we upgraded to SQL Anywhere 17 we created a new schema named FAST_UL, in the same database. We only ran syncora.sql under the new FAST_UL schema and nothing else. We've set it up this way as our clients need to sync with Sybase 9 and 17. There are two different Mobilink services with each using a different user/schema. One uses FAST while the other uses FAST_UL. We haven't had any problems with this configuration at our client sites, well we do gat a few ML_GET_BLOCKED_INFO errors every now and then but they seem to resolve themselves. All of our development configurations use our Azure VM using an Amazon hosted database. We have other test clients set up the same way for development purposes, but only one is causing us major headaches with getting this error constantly. We have compared databases, Mobilink services, ODBC connections, etc., and they are all the same (but with different databases of course). The ODBC DSN and Mobilink service are set up to use FAST_UL when using Sybase 17. Again, we did not run upgrade_ora.sql as my colleague thinks that running syncora.sql is sufficient. He's also worried about other tables being affected, like ml_user, but I'm not sure why he cares. Should we go ahead and run upgrade_ora.sql under our FAST_UL schema? Would we need to run syncora.sql again after that? Also re-compiling the ML_GET_BLOCKED_INFO did not resolve the problem.

entry.title: SAP IBP error -To use the PLM Engine, a reactivation of planning area is required
entry.content:
Activated global parameter-USE_NEW_PLM_ENGINE = YES;USER_GROUP_NEW_PLM_ENGINE=ALL

After this change, all planning area created before this change have error-To use the PLM Engine, a reactivation of planning area is required When Statistical forecasting or Time Series Analysis is Run.

I already tried reactivating PA as suggested in error but the error still continues in SAP IBP.

entry.title: Crystal Reports 13.0.27 Issue: PDF Conversion and Print View Not Working (August 2024) Windows 10
entry.content:
Dear all,

My application, developed with Visual Studio 2019 and using Crystal Reports version 13.0.27, suddenly stopped working correctly as of August 2024.

Currently, it no longer converts prints to PDF, and in some cases, the view where the print buttons are located is not loading anymore. The operating system running the application is Windows 10.

Has anyone else encountered similar issues?

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: Production 2 line process
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a First process which manufacture the Tire(Production line-1), once tire is produced then only can make the Car(FG).(Production line -2).

May i know how can i map this process in SAP connecting together?

Thanks,

Shri

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Identity Services logon against SAP ECC or S4...
entry.content: Unfortunately the backend has to be my user store.  My other comment for more details.  First timer!

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Identity Services logon against SAP ECC or S4...
entry.content: Using the backend as the user store is my requirement. Our BTP implementation is but a very small part of a large on-premise solution. Suffice to say, it's a limitation on my end. I should mention that SAP ECC 8.0/S4 does have a SAML2 module that is included and can be activated. I tried this route, but was unsuccessful in having SAP Identity Services leverage that SAML server as a user store.

entry.title: #COE#BUMA Using Teams and Responsibility on both PR and PO Workflows
entry.content:
Hello,

 
Good day!

We aim to design and utilize the Teams and Responsibility app for both PR and PO workflows without having to create separate team functions for each. Is it feasible to use just the Responsibility definition filters to differentiate between PR and PO while assigning team members through a single team function?

If so, could you guide us on how to achieve this?

Thank you and regards!

John

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Identity Services logon against SAP ECC or S4...
entry.content: Thanks for your response! I'm not trying to use the ECC logon page as an authentication mechanism, but rather use SAP ECC as the central user store. My initial approach was to activate the SAML2 module in SAP ECC, and then use SAP CLOUD Identity Services to leverage this SAML2 server as the system to authenticate against. This didn't go very far after I followed a couple blogs (check https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/enabling-saml-single-sign-on-for-sap-s-4-hana-and-sap-btp-apps-using-the/ba-p/13694598). However, I wasn't able to progress much here, I feel like either the blog post mis-represents what its trying to do or my limited SAML2 knowledge won't let me get a working solution. That said, is it your understanding that I should be able to activate SAML2 in SAP ECC and use it through SAP Cloud Identity Services?

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Identity Services logon against SAP ECC or S4 backend?

entry.title: Re: ML_GET_BLOCKED_INFO
entry.content:
The ML_GET_BLOCKED_INFO error indicates "wrong number or types of arguments in call to 'ML_GET_BLOCKED_INFO'" so what would be calling it with the wrong number of parameters? Under our Sybase 9 schema (FAST) there is no ML_GET_BLOCKED_INFO but I could've sworn I saw one with only two parameters whereas the Sybase 17 version has two paramters.

Just curoius as to what's up with the wrong number of parameters, as well as everything else.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: ML_GET_BLOCKED_INFO
entry.content: I meant that the Sybase 17 uses three parameters, not two.

entry.title: Onboarding USA I-9 Compliance Section 1 - Unable to Fetch Form Data
entry.content:
But SuccessFactors:

  • After the 2H 2022 release, updated the Form I-9 Section 1 verification step so that it now includes the complete phone number (including area code) collected in the personal data collection step.
  • If the format is incorrect, a validation message that prompts the new hires to enter their phone number in the correct format. Hence, Area Code should be enabled.

But, if the Area Code in EC Phone Information portlet is disabled, when the onboardee goes to complete the I9 Step1 process, the system throws an error: Unable to Fetch Form Data. (Refer to my earlier blog on the similar topic/issue: Onboarding I9 Process failing due to missing area code in phone number field (USA Compliance Process only) 

To mitigate this error, many onboarding customers enable the "Area Code" in EC. This means, in the Onboarding Data Collection process step, the onboadee will be required to enter the Area Code, and the Phone Number in a separate field.

This configuration is cumbersome for the following reasons:

- When the customer enable the area code to support onboarding, it has a downstream impact - data cleansing to the existing phone data in EC, and possibly EC related integrations

- If the customer enable the area code field but, continue to collect the full phone information in the phone-number field, it does provide a good user experience during data collection - the onboadee need to be notified not to enter the area code, or enter the area code, and re-enter the area-code+phone-number in the phone-number field.

To overcome this limitation, customers and implementation teams can consider configuring workaround in the system.

This blog, provides step-by-step instructions on what configuration is required to support this workaround.

Configuration

Manage Data

Goto Manage Data. In Create New, enter FieldMetaOverrideConfiguration

Enter the ID as whatever you prefer; for example – ONB_overRide_PhAreaCode

Change the overrideFields value to Yes

In field supportedTypes enter US_I9

In field metaOverrideType select the option Form Metadata Override

In the section overrideFieldMetadatas for the field id, enter phoneInfo.areaCode

In the field source, select the option Prepopulated Employee Central Fields Metadata

Leave the field oldFieldsMeta blank

In the field overrideFieldsMeta enter the below code as-is

{
"entity": "phoneInfo",
"entityPath":"personNav/phoneNav",
"field": "phoneNumber",
"filters": {
"phoneType":"ENABLED"
}
}

Click Save.

After configuration the table should look similar to the figure below

 

 

Configuration to be made

 

 Testing Process:

Test by re-starting by an existing onboarding record or create a new onboarding record, and complete the compliance step: I9 Section 1.

Conclusion:
This configuration will retain the phone number that you have entered in the personal data collection step in the phoneInformation section. The phone number that is entered, will be transferred in full to the I9 form, and no additional step need to be completed by the onboardee.

With this workaround, you do not enhance or change the existing EC configuration or even create a new Onboardee person type in phoneInformation

Do leave comments below, if you have other work arounds.

Reference: SAP KBA: 3264952 - Unable to Fetch Form Data Error for USA I9 Compliance form after 2H 2022 Release - Onboarding

entry.title: Re: Onboarding USA I-9 Compliance Section 1 - Unable to Fetch Form Data
entry.content: I wrote this post. Unfortunately, SCN were not able to migrate this post. I have emails correspondence as well. I have re-published this post, and you can find them here: Onboarding USA I-9 Compliance Section 1 - Unable to Fetch Form Data (Re-Post)

entry.title: Re: WBS - Project with Revenue - Margin Analysis not updated
entry.content:
Hi Sandeep,

It's difficult to get the cause without system access.

Maybe you can consider to check the filter setting for the app.

Also compare with Journal Entry Analyzer for the actual journal entries created.

Best Regards,
Owen

entry.title: Re: Unable to Create the GL account groups in the CBC
entry.content:
The account group should start with only letters 'Z' or 'Y', no other letters are allowed.

Best Regards,
Owen

entry.title: Re: Increase decimals in currency
entry.content:
Unfortunately in Public Cloud system at present, it is not supported to change decimal places for currency codes which is the similar configuration as OY04 in on-premise.

Best Regards,
Owen

entry.title: Change Default mta.yaml filepath in BTP CICD pipeline config.yaml
entry.content:
I have a full stack multi-tenant project. This repository has two MTA's. One is for the CAP related artifacts which is in the root directory. The other is inside the app folder which holds the artifacts related to the frontend(UI5). 

Now I need to setup my pipeline in BTP CICD. I added the github repository and tried to create a job. 
Herein I understood, if I select buildtool as mta, it searches for mta.yaml file in the root directory of repository. 
The thing is I need to setup two jobs one should pick mta in root directory and deploy in provider subaccount and other should pick mta from app folder and deploy it in subscriber subaccount. 

Is there any other option which we can set in config.yaml file inside .pipeline folder which will override the default mta.yaml filepath or atleast set the mta file name like mta-ui.yaml ??


@Nikitha09 

or is there any other way to achieve this?

SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery

entry.title: Re: FORM TEMPLATE FOR OUTGOING CHECK IN SAP S/4HANA, PUBLIC CLOUD

entry.title: Re: FORM TEMPLATE FOR OUTGOING CHECK IN SAP S/4HANA, PUBLIC CLOUD
entry.content: Informed expert.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud - Hide prices on invoice
entry.content:
Hi @rubenpeijs ,

In SD sales documents and Billing documents we have a concept called Role based access to Price elements this functionality can be used for sales order or in Billing documents to control the access to the condition in SD documents. The following access can be defined to a condition No access, display only and change access based on the Business role.
Use the No access to prevent the display of the condition to certain business roles or for all business roles.

Refer the SAP document for more info:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/bf9f70beb3bb4258be53846b6363c52b.htm

Hope this helps!!!

Thanks and Regards,
Suryaprakash.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud for planning - Advanced Formula "LINK" is not working
entry.content: I would suggest you to use trace feature within DA designer to see if there is any data read

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024

entry.content:

Reservation /Pick list qty with UOM based on Production orders

Posting against Pick list from stores with Qty/UOM

Consumption against Production order with Qty/UOM

entry.title: Re: Exported the Model did some changes, which is not ref...
entry.content: There might be nothing wrong there.  The first screenshot shows your updated fact( data of year 2023 and 2024) through upload . But that doesn't change the master data of date dimension. In your second screenshot, you can also see the valid time range of your date dimension is from 2020 to 2024. So you should see member of 2021 in your date dimension.

entry.title: Re: Sales Invoices for Export sales with payment of IGST...
entry.content:
Hi @Bhavesh_Bhorde ,

From the above question I can understand that we need to add the IGST, condition for the export sales only for some customer right or we will have IGST only some sales orders even when the customer is same? or pls correct me if am missing something.

If the control is required based on the customers that is only some customer will have IGST then we can use the TAX category IGST with classification(0 or 1).

The tax percentage will be determined based on the material's IGST tax category classification value and customer's IGST tax category classification value 0 and 1.
We can maintain in the condition record whenever the customer's IGST Tax category classification is 0, irrespective of the material tax category the Tax percentage should be Zero.

Tax classification needs to be entered in the Sales Areas --> Taxes --> Tax category IGST or in Sales org data --> Billing Tab.

Hope this helps!!!!

Thanks and Regards,
Surya.

entry.title: Re: How to activate test run option from MDS_LOAD_COCKPIT
entry.content: You can enable the test run functionality by implementing SAP Note 2874184

entry.title: Re: How can I print a check after issuing it using the 'P...
entry.content:
Hi,

Thank you for your prompt response. I hope you are doing well.

The note you referred to, KBA 3141420, appears to address issues related to reprinting voided or cashed cheques, which does not seem to apply to my current situation. My concern is not about voided or cashed cheques but rather about printing a cheque for a payment that I made through the "Post Outgoing Payment" function. could not find any option to print the cheque. Could you please guide me on how to print the cheque for the payment document in this scenario? Looking forward to your guidance.

entry.title: Which way of S4 Hana system implementation is suggested, if client is new to SAP and or old to SAP?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Ref. to subject mentioned. can you through some light on it.!

Because, yesterday I have attended an interview for SAP Head position in Hyd in a product based company especially a Manufacturing industry, they were saying they are using sap from past 20 years (this is a bid wonder?), but the company is 30 years old. and recently 2 years ago they have to SAP S4 Hana system.

However still they are not happy, says that they have lot of gaps in their system after implementing SAP S4Hana, not sure, if they have implemented green field implementation (meaning from the scratch, need to build the organization into new S4 Hana ERP system, Fiori based).

And also said that, they have these concerns mentioning below: 

-1). People issue (might be their company people /business have not cooperated and or supported during migration)

-2). Data inputs have not given properly,

-3). Systems issues

feeling, all these because the work is not done properly in the first 3 phases of Activate methodology. Discover, Prepare and Explore phases were not fully utilized, meaning the activities and or task were not well taken care, is it not?

Otherwise, they have now still gaps, and the management were not happy, cause they reports or dash board are not meeting as per their expectation, due to that, management is not able to take proper decision and or they is a delay in that or lagging... resulting in that, they are not satisfied it seems. And they said that, it as implemented by a Pune based sap implementation partner.!

but one thing asked since they are old to sap 20 years, still their system is not fully stabilized.. so is it not because of something else, that is just influence of owners? because its a private limited company. One man army show, so feeling sap have developed a methodology to utilize and implement the new system for their industry for better way of digitization of their enterprise right?  And the results are as expected, provided when the companies follow better, and leverage all the best practices and implemented, right?

So, need your expert opinion, what might be the causes, best possible suggestions.!

Appreciate your expertise thoughts.!

Thanks in advance,

Regards.

Er.K.S.

entry.title: Subsequent adjustment of Transfer Price in PCA Valuation
entry.content:
In chemical industry certain raw material price is controlled via government authorities. These prices are only published at the month end. Goods are transferred between Profit Centres using Transfer Price in Profit Center Valuation.

At the month end customer updates the transfer price based on new information received.

The requirement is to revaluate material movements between profit centres with a new determined transfer price at month end. At the moment no standard solution is available for this requirement.

Material Ledger with Actual costing is implemented and Raw Materials has price control 'S'.

Please do let us know if any custom solution can be implemented for such scenarios or already implemented by you. The guidance will be appreciated. 

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Crystal Report Viewer Chart Font Issue
entry.content:
Got the Report, right, it doesn't have the ability to add attachment either but I got it from your drop box.

I noticed you don't have any printer selected so it's using GDIPlus32.dll to format and some of your default printer properties.

It can cause issues moving the report from PC to PC, so always highly recommended to use a real printer.

Once you do that send me the new report.

I do see there is a difference.

Go into Page Setup and select a real printer, save the report and test again. Check on Dissociate also.

Then when you select File, Print you'll see a printer saved and listed on the dialog box I used my default:

I'll send it off to R&D and see what they have to say...

Don

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports 13.0.27 Issue: PDF Conversion and Pri...
entry.content:
Likely some Windows update caused the problem.

Is Windows 10 even supported anymore, quick check it is for another year:

Important

Windows 10 will reach end of support on October 14, 2025. The current version, 22H2, will be the final version of Windows 10, and all editions will remain in support with monthly security update releases through that date. Existing LTSC releases will continue to receive updates beyond that date based on their specific lifecycles.

Try SP 36 which you can download from here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

See my blog on upgrading your project:

Upgrading a Visual Studio 2008->2022 .NET project with the latest CR .NET SDK packages

entry.title: BYD2408 Remittance Advice form template sender selection
entry.content:
Hi SAP Support!


To my understanding, the sender logic varies by document type, but in general the system selects the sender with this hierarchy, where if a sender can't be found at one step it falls back to the next:
1. Document-level rules. *I believe Remittance Advices send based on the owner of the original document the RA is related to but don't see off-the-shelf documentation to confirm this*
2. Output Channel select rules.
3. Default sender maintained in Business Configuration.

Can you confirm if this is correct, and if so what the logic is behind the document level sender selection rules for Remittance Advices and where it might be found in the off-the-shelf documentation for ByDesign?

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BYD2408 Remittance Advice form template sender selection
entry.content:
The system determines the sender of a remittance advice using a hierarchical logic, ensuring flexibility and control over how communication is sent:

  1. Document-Level Sender: By default, the sender is typically the owner of the document that triggered the remittance advice, such as the payment run owner or invoice owner.

  2. Output Channel Rules: You can configure the output channel (e.g., email) to use a specific sender address, like a generic AP email. This rule overrides the document-level owner and allows for centralized email communication.

  3. Default Sender: If neither of the above applies, the system will use the default sender email address defined in the system's Business Configuration settings.

By adjusting the output channel rules, we can ensure that all remittance advices are sent from a predefined generic AP email rather than the individual document owner.
